steps.title: Separate two clauses
steps.content: The first clause says efficiency improvements often correlate negatively with resource conservation. The second clause says efficiency gains may lower costs and increase demand. Because both sides can stand as complete sentences, the punctuation needs a strong boundary between them.

steps.title: Place though correctly
steps.content: A leaves though awkwardly starting the second clause, while B and C rely on commas and create a comma splice. The correct punctuation lets though trail the first clause, then uses a semicolon before the next independent clause. The answer is D.
